evals.name: stale-closure
evals.prompt: This Counter component should increment every second but it's stuck at 1. The console.log shows 'tick, current count: 0' every time. I tried adding `count` to the useEffect dependency array but then it creates a new interval every render and the count jumps by huge amounts. I also tried wrapping the setInterval callback in useCallback and passing count as a dependency — same rapid-fire problem. What's going on and how do I fix this?
evals.expected_output: Structured hypothesis-driven analysis identifying stale closure, with functional updater fix
expectations: Lists at least 2 distinct hypotheses for what could be causing the bug
Proposes a diagnostic step or experiment before jumping to the final fix
Explains what evidence confirms the root cause before applying the fix
Correctly identifies stale closure over the count variable as the root cause
Proposes using the functional updater form (setCount(prev => prev + 1) or equivalent) as the fix

evals.name: css-overflow
evals.prompt: My Card component is supposed to have rounded corners (border-radius: 12px is in the CSS) but they're completely square when rendered on the dashboard page. I added border-radius: 12px !important to the .card class — still square. I also tried adding border-radius directly to the Card component as an inline style — no change. The corners are just flat. The weird thing is if I render the Card in isolation (not inside the DashboardLayout), the corners show up fine.
evals.expected_output: Structured hypothesis-driven analysis identifying overflow:hidden on parent, with fix to Layout.css
expectations: Lists at least 2 distinct hypotheses for what could be causing the bug
Proposes a diagnostic step or experiment before jumping to the final fix
Explains what evidence confirms the root cause before applying the fix
Correctly identifies overflow: hidden on the parent .content-area as the cause of clipped corners
Fix modifies Layout.css rather than Card.css

evals.name: async-race
evals.prompt: Our userService.js has a nasty intermittent bug. Sometimes when two users hit the API at nearly the same time, user A gets user B's permissions, or vice versa. The logging middleware (which calls getCurrentUser()) sometimes logs the wrong user too. I added null checks for the user object — didn't help, the user IS there, it's just the wrong user. I also wrapped the whole getEnrichedUser in a try/catch thinking it was a failed fetch — no errors are thrown, the data is just wrong. This only happens under concurrent load, never in single-user testing.
evals.expected_output: Structured hypothesis-driven analysis identifying shared mutable state race condition, with local variable fix
expectations: Lists at least 2 distinct hypotheses for what could be causing the bug
Proposes a diagnostic step or experiment before jumping to the final fix
Explains what evidence confirms the root cause before applying the fix
Correctly identifies the shared mutable currentUser module-level variable as the race condition
Fix removes or localizes the module-level state so concurrent calls don't interfere
